reviewerNotes: Reviewed commit 8ba7360 implementing Marcus Aurelius persona, session state machine, and AI-generated summaries. Three new services (persona.ts, sessionStateMachine.ts, sessionSummary.ts) with comprehensive unit tests (62 tests across 3 test files). Chat route integrated personalized greeting injection; new /api/sessions/:id/end-and-summarize endpoint added. All tests pass, typecheck clean, lint clean. Non-blocking issues: (1) getStoicContextForTopic untested, (2) greeting logic duplicated between persona.ts and chat.ts, (3) test files use 'as any' against project conventions, (4) state machine permits intro→summary direct transition bypassing expected flow.

issues.description: getStoicContextForTopic() uses synchronous require() to import content.js but has no test coverage. If the content service changes or fails, this function breaks silently. Should add a test case.

buildChatMessages() in chat.ts duplicates greeting injection logic that already exists in buildConversationContext() in persona.ts. The persona.ts version is more complete (handles stoic context), but chat.ts does its own version. Consider reusing buildConversationContext to avoid drift.

Tests use '(summaryService as any)' to access private parse methods, which violates the project convention of avoiding 'any'. Could export parse methods as internal utilities or use a testing-friendly interface.

VALID_TRANSITIONS allows intro→summary direct transition, which bypasses the intended conversation flow (intro→active→closing→summary described in feature and architecture docs). This is technically valid for edge cases but could result in sessions that skip conversation entirely.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Test files for sessionSummary use 'as any' to access private methods, which conflicts with AGENTS.md TypeScript convention to 'avoid any'. The test convention guidance in AGENTS.md does not address this pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/services/sessionSummary.test.ts:134-171 — multiple cases cast summaryService to 'any' to access parseSummaryResponse and parseActionItemsResponse

sharedStateObservations.observation: The end-and-summarize endpoint calls getSessionSummaryService() which uses a singleton. If the endpoint is called multiple times in the same process lifetime with different service instances (e.g., test vs production), the singleton may hold stale references. The worker fixed this in the constructor pattern but the endpoint still uses the zero-arg singleton accessor.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/routes/session.ts:163 — getSessionSummaryService() called with no args, relies on singleton that was created before services were potentially reset

summary: Feature implementation is solid and covers all four expected behaviors: personalized greeting, stoic conversation, summary generation, and action item extraction. Three new services with 62 passing tests, clean typecheck and lint. The session state machine correctly manages intro→active→closing→summary transitions with proper validation. AI summary generation includes robust fallback for offline Ollama. Four non-blocking issues identified (untested function, duplicated greeting logic, 'as any' in tests, permissive state transitions) — none require rework before proceeding.
